question: Will Raphinha be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 La Liga season?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 La Liga season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 La Liga season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Goals scored in La Liga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., Copa del Rey, Spanish Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 La Liga season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from La Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappe's overwhelming 92.8% implied probability as La Liga top goalscorer reflects his elite finishing and Real Madrid's high-powered attack, amplified by a recent hat-trick in a 5-0 rout of Real Betis that boosted his tally to 9 goals through 13 matches. question: Will any 2026 FIFA World Cup game scheduled in the U.S. be relocated abroad?
description:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is scheduled to begin on Thursday, June 11, and conclude on Sunday, July 19. More information about the match schedule can be found here (https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/match-schedule-fixtures-results-teams-stadiums). According to ESPN, there have been complaints about the summertime heat across America, causing FIFA to consider relocating the 2026 World Cup out of the United States.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is announced that any game originally scheduled to be held in the U.S. to be relocated to a location outside of the U.S. by June 10, 2026, 11:59 ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement will immediately resolve the market to "Yes" regardless of whether the relocation is later revoked. 

The resolution source will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: FIFA's unchanged confirmation of 16 North American venues since the 2022 announcement anchors trader sentiment at 85.6% against relocation, as all 11 U.S.-scheduled matches—from group stage openers at SoFi Stadium to the Rose Bowl—are progressing on track with stadium expansions and certifications. No official reports from FIFA, U.S. Soccer, or host cities indicate logistical hurdles, security risks, or infrastructure delays severe enough to shift games abroad, especially amid committed multimillion-dollar upgrades at sites like MetLife and Mercedes-Benz Stadium. Recent site visits and FIFA inspections affirm readiness, underscoring the low-probability event of upending the host nation framework for the expanded 48-team tournament.

This is a polymarket to predict which clubs are relegated from Serie A after the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club is officially relegated by Serie A following the 2025–26 season, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from Serie A. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Venezia, Hellas Verona, and Lecce anchor trader consensus as Serie A relegation favorites, rooted in their dire standings—11th, 12th, and 15 points after 15 matches, trailing safety by 6-10 points. Recent developments amplify risks: Verona sacked coach Paolo Zanetti amid a six-game winless skid, Venezia conceded 17 goals in eight outings despite a gritty Parma draw, and Lecce battles without key midfielder Remi Oudin (hamstring). Empoli and Cagliari lurk close above, buoyed by recent form, but all face grueling schedules including clashes with Inter, Juventus, and Milan. Historical data shows 80% of bottom-three teams relegate, validating crowd wisdom amid momentum shifts and upset potential.

question: Will Ousmane Dembele be the top goal scorer in the 2025-26 Ligue 1 season?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Only goals scored in Ligue 1 matches will count. Goals scored in other competitions (e.g., Coupe de France, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Ligue 1. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mason Greenwood's explosive start dominates trader consensus for Ligue 1 top goalscorer, with his seven goals—including a hat-trick in Marseille's 5-0 rout of St Etienne on October 6—propelling 44.5% implied probability after just seven matches. Esteban Lepaul follows at 25.9% on five goals for his side, buoyed by consistent finishing amid Lens's solid form, while Joaquin Panichelli's four tallies for Reims earn 19.7% on breakout pace from the young forward. Desire Doue's three goals and creativity at Rennes support 14.4%, and Ousmane Dembele's four strikes yield 7.0% despite PSG's shared attack. Early-season momentum favors leaders, but fixture difficulty and depth could shift odds as the campaign intensifies.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Only goals scored in Ligue 1 matches will count. Goals scored in other competitions (e.g., Coupe de France, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Ligue 1. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mason Greenwood's explosive start dominates trader consensus for Ligue 1 top goalscorer, with his seven goals—including a hat-trick in Marseille's 5-0 rout of St Etienne on October 6—propelling 44.5% implied probability after just seven matches. Esteban Lepaul follows at 25.9% on five goals for his side, buoyed by consistent finishing amid Lens's solid form, while Joaquin Panichelli's four tallies for Reims earn 19.7% on breakout pace from the young forward. Desire Doue's three goals and creativity at Rennes support 14.4%, and Ousmane Dembele's four strikes yield 7.0% despite PSG's shared attack. Early-season momentum favors leaders, but fixture difficulty and depth could shift odds as the campaign intensifies.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Only goals scored in Ligue 1 matches will count. Goals scored in other competitions (e.g., Coupe de France, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Ligue 1. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mason Greenwood's explosive start dominates trader consensus for Ligue 1 top goalscorer, with his seven goals—including a hat-trick in Marseille's 5-0 rout of St Etienne on October 6—propelling 44.5% implied probability after just seven matches. Esteban Lepaul follows at 25.9% on five goals for his side, buoyed by consistent finishing amid Lens's solid form, while Joaquin Panichelli's four tallies for Reims earn 19.7% on breakout pace from the young forward. Desire Doue's three goals and creativity at Rennes support 14.4%, and Ousmane Dembele's four strikes yield 7.0% despite PSG's shared attack. Early-season momentum favors leaders, but fixture difficulty and depth could shift odds as the campaign intensifies.

events.title: UEFA Europa League: Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Europa League.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Aston Villa at 32% implied probability to win the UEFA Europa League, driven by their dominant league phase finish atop the standings with 22 points and a commanding 6-1 aggregate knockout playoff win over Club Brugge, positioning them favorably in the round of 16 against Athletic Bilbao. Porto (15.3%) and Real Betis (14%) follow, bolstered by Porto's experienced European pedigree and Betis' resilient 4-3 aggregate victory over Fenerbahce amid strong La Liga form. Nottingham Forest and Celta Vigo (both 8.6%) surged via playoff upsets, while Bologna, Freiburg, and Braga reflect solid group stage momentum and home advantages in upcoming ties; no major injuries reported across leaders shift odds significantly yet.

question: Will Lautaro Martinez be the top goal scorer in the 2025-26 Serie A season?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Serie A season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Serie A season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Only goals scored in Serie A matches will count. Goals scored in other competitions (e.g., Coppa Italia, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

This is a polymarket to predict which player is drafted #1 overall in the 2026 NFL Draft.

If the listed player is drafted first overall in the 2026 NFL Draft, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 NFL Draft is canceled or not completed by July 30, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fernando Mendoza's prototypical 6'5" frame, rocket arm, and No. 1 recruiting pedigree as a five-star Cal quarterback prospect anchor his 95.3% implied probability as the 2026 NFL Draft's top pick, per early mock drafts from PFF and ESPN projecting him to teams like the Titans amid QB-needy franchises. Trader consensus reflects his elite tools mirroring past No. 1s like Caleb Williams, outpacing South Carolina's LaNorris Sellers or others lacking similar hype. Challenges include Mendoza's freshman growing pains in a tough Pac-12, injuries, or juniors like Carson Beck declaring early and dominating, plus upstart seasons from Arch Manning or Nico Iamaleava shifting the class hierarchy.

question: Will Manchester City win the 2025–26 Carabao Cup?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 Carabao Cup.

If the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 Carabao Cup, this market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the 2025–26 Carabao Cup is canceled or not completed by June 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official results published by the Carabao Cup / EFL Cup (https://www.efl.com/competitions/carabao-cup/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 Carabao Cup.

If the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 Carabao Cup, this market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the 2025–26 Carabao Cup is canceled or not completed by June 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official results published by the Carabao Cup / EFL Cup (https://www.efl.com/competitions/carabao-cup/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's stranglehold on the Carabao Cup winner market stems from their flawless progression through the tournament, including emphatic semi-final victories over Aston Villa, showcasing squad depth and clinical finishing even amid Premier League title pressures. As defending champions under Pep Guardiola with a history of six wins in 11 years, traders price them at near-certainty (100.0%) reflecting superior home form, rest advantages, and Haaland's goal-scoring prowess. Arsenal's faint 0.1% implies elimination or long-shot status, but realistic challenges include Man City injuries to Rodri or De Bruyne, fatigue from Champions League clashes, or a final upset akin to Tottenham's past giant-killings, though historical data favors the Citizens in knockouts.

question: Will Arsenal finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool's eight wins in 11 matches anchor the top of the Premier League table at 28 points, fueling trader consensus for their near-certain top-four finish, while Arsenal's unbeaten run (25 points) keeps them close behind. Chelsea's surge under Enzo Maresca—six wins in 11, including recent triumphs over top sides—has propelled them to third (22 points), boosting their implied probabilities amid a favorable run-in. Manchester City's slide to fourth (21 points) stems from Rodri's long-term absence and back-to-back dropped points against Bournemouth and Liverpool, heightening vulnerability despite Haaland's goals. Upcoming Champions League midweeks and fixtures like Chelsea vs. Arsenal could widen gaps, with historical late surges underscoring the race's volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's explosive 10 wins in 12 La Liga matches have vaulted them to the top with 30 points, fueling trader consensus on their top-four lock for Champions League qualification, especially after a dominant 4-0 Clasico rout of Real Madrid. Madrid sits second at 27 points but grapples with key absences like Carvajal and Militao sidelined long-term, exposing defensive vulnerabilities amid recent draws. Atletico Madrid (26 points) and Athletic Bilbao (23 points) lurk closely, bolstered by Griezmann's form and Bilbao's home strength, though both face grueling schedules including Madrid derbies and rival clashes before the World Cup break. Momentum favors Barca, but upsets remain likely in this compressed race.
